label: true-dup
a: JWT refresh tokens are now single-use. Once a client uses a refresh token to get a new access token, the refresh token is immediately invalidated in Redis. A fresh refresh token is issued alongside the new access token. If someone steals a refresh token and tries to use it after the legitimate client has already used it, the invalidated token is rejected.
b: Established the rule that each refresh token can only be used once. When the client sends a refresh token to get a new access token, two things happen atomically: the old refresh token is added to a Redis revocation set, and a new refresh token is generated and returned to the client. A revoked token is refused on any subsequent use.
reviewer_notes: Both describe single-use refresh tokens with atomic revocation+reissue in Redis.
